summary: Closed, unmerged PR that improved lbarq decompilation by giving the ARQ node pool and state machine concrete types, replacing magic state/list indices with named enum values, and applying targeted matching controls such as scoped dont_inline and PAD_STACK. The PR body reported 100% matches for lbArq_80014ABC and lbArq_80014D2C, with lbArq_80014AC4 and lbArq_80014BD0 still partially drifting; the automated decomp.dev report confirmed lbArq_80014BD0 improved from 93.97% to 96.55%.

key_files.role: Implementation changes for lbarq state transitions, node-pool initialization, matching pragmas, and stack padding.
Header changes adding lbArqState, typing lbArqNode.state, and replacing lbArqGlobal opaque padding with nodes[10].

what_changed: Added typedef enum lbArqState with LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE = 0, LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING = 1, and LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE = 2.
Changed lbArqNode.state from u32 to lbArqState and changed lbArq_80014ABC to return lbArqState instead of u32.
Replaced lbArqGlobal's opaque u8 pad[0x1E0] with lbArqNode nodes[10], while keeping list[3] at offset 0x1E0.
Updated lbArq_80014AC4, lbArq_80014BD0, and lbArq_80014D2C to use LB_ARQ_STATE_* constants instead of raw list/state values 0, 1, and 2.
Updated lbArq_80014D2C initialization to use global->nodes instead of casting the global object to lbArqNode*.
Wrapped lbArq_80014ABC in scoped #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop to keep the helper out-of-line.
Added PAD_STACK(16) in lbArq_80014BD0 to improve stack/codegen matching.
Reported matching results: lbArq_80014ABC 100%, lbArq_80014D2C 100%, lbArq_80014AC4 96.9403%, and lbArq_80014BD0 96.55173%.
smart_moves: Replacing opaque global padding with a real 10-node lbArqNode pool improved type knowledge without changing the documented offset of the list array.
Using enum constants preserved the verified numeric state values while making the relationship between node state and global->list indexing explicit.
The scoped dont_inline pragma was narrowly applied to lbArq_80014ABC, avoiding a broad compiler-behavior change while preserving the intended out-of-line call pattern.
PAD_STACK(16) was used as a localized matching knob in lbArq_80014BD0 after structural cleanup, rather than obscuring the logic.
The PR documented both successful matches and remaining drift causes, making future work easier to target.
decomp_lessons: When a padded global is understood, replace the padding with concrete arrays or structs while preserving offset comments and layout constraints.
If a state value doubles as a list index, encode the exact numeric values in an enum so the semantics are clearer and codegen can stay equivalent.
Use scoped pragmas such as #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline/#pragma pop when a small helper must remain out-of-line for matching.
PAD_STACK can be useful for stack/codegen tuning, but remaining drift should still be documented rather than hidden.
Function-by-function checkdiff output is valuable context: here it separated fully matched helpers from lbArq_80014AC4 and lbArq_80014BD0, which still had known drift.
Because this PR was closed unmerged and has no human review comments in the slice, treat it as useful matching evidence rather than accepted final project state.
naming_conventions: New enum type uses project-style mixed-case typedef naming: lbArqState.
Enum constants use uppercase subsystem-prefixed names: LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE, LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING, LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE.
Existing address-based symbol names were retained for unmatched/unnamed functions and globals, such as lbArq_80014ABC and lbArq_804316C0.
Struct fields use lower_snake_case for descriptive members such as callback_arg, while short established fields like next, state, arq, nodes, and list remain simple.
Comments were simplified from explicit list indices, such as list[2], to semantic names like Add to done list once enum constants represented the indices.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma dont_inline specifically to lbArq_80014ABC to keep a small state getter emitted as a callable function.
Inserted PAD_STACK(16) in lbArq_80014BD0 to improve stack/codegen matching.
Kept PAD_STACK(8) in lbArq_80014D2C while changing initialization to use global->nodes.
Preserved enum values 0, 1, and 2 so replacements for raw list indices should not change state/list semantics.
Verified with python tools/checkdiff.py for lbArq_80014ABC, lbArq_80014AC4, lbArq_80014BD0, and lbArq_80014D2C, plus python configure.py && ninja.
Remaining documented drift: lbArq_80014AC4 had one first-list address calculation order issue; lbArq_80014BD0 had saved-register allocation drift plus one extra move around the free-list head.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed one unmatched-item improvement: main/melee/lb/lbarq lbArq_80014BD0 improved by +10 bytes, from 93.97% before to 96.55% after.
PR was closed without merge, so downstream reuse should verify whether the same changes landed in a later PR.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for lbarq, lbArqState, or LB_ARQ_STATE_* to see whether this closed work was superseded or merged elsewhere.
Investigate the remaining lbArq_80014AC4 first-list address calculation order mismatch.
Investigate lbArq_80014BD0 saved-register allocation drift and the extra move around the free-list head.
Compare other library ARQ matching PRs that use PAD_STACK or scoped #pragma dont_inline for small helper functions.
